question.text: When a commodity futures position is used for hedging and the hedged item is raw material inventory, the hedge gain/loss is:

answer.text: Adjusted against the cost of inventory — matching the hedge P&L with the underlying exposure

explanation.text: **The Logic:** In hedge accounting, the hedge gain/loss is matched with the hedged item — if hedging raw material inventory, the hedge P&L adjusts the inventory cost. **The Trap:** Not separate investment income. Not ignored. Not extraordinary. The principle is matching. **Cross-Exam Context:** Hedge accounting matching principle applies across markets. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Proper hedge accounting smooths income statements — instead of showing volatile derivative P&L, the hedge result is matched with the underlying business transaction it protects.
